title: [Bug] Owners cannot auto-reserve subdomains when AllowClientAutoReservation is disabled
body:
**Issue Analysis:**
Currently, the CLI client fails to auto-reserve subdomains (e.g., `peters-mbp`) for users with `owner` or `admin` roles if the global server setting `AllowClientAutoReservation` is disabled. The server returns: `Custom subdomains must be reserved in the portal prior to connecting`.

Upon reviewing `pkg/server/server.go`, the condition in `handleRegister` and `handleEdgeRegister` strictly requires both `AllowClientAutoReservation` AND an admin/owner role (`s.cfg.AllowClientAutoReservation && userRec != nil && (userRec.Role == "admin" || userRec.Role == "owner")`).

**Implementation Plan:**
1. Modify the condition in `handleRegister` (in `pkg/server/server.go`) to use `||` instead of `&&` so that Admin/Owner roles can bypass the global `AllowClientAutoReservation` restriction.
2. Apply the same logic fix to `handleEdgeRegister`.

issues.title: Fix AllowClientAutoReservation logic in server handlers
issues.body: Update `handleRegister` and `handleEdgeRegister` in `pkg/server/server.go` to correctly evaluate the logical OR condition for Admin/Owner auto-reservation overrides.
